skill.name: lead-scraper
skill.description: Generate structured B2B prospect lists by region and industry, exported as CSV with company name, contact, phone, and address fields.
skill.summary: Assist in building industry lead lists for sales outreach.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: The skill consists of a single SKILL.md descriptor with no executable code, network calls, or external commands. Both static findings (weak cryptographic algorithm and high file entropy) are false positives caused by Chinese text and the literal string SKILL.md. A minor ethical note is recorded because the described use case involves collecting personal contact data, which requires compliance with privacy laws.

low_findings.title: Personal contact data collection mentioned in description
low_findings.description: The skill description states the goal of producing company contact, phone, and address records. The file contains no scraping logic, but operators must comply with privacy laws such as GDPR and PIPL when using the resulting prompts. This is an advisory note, not a code finding.

content.user_title: Build Targeted B2B Lead Lists by Region and Industry
content.value_statement: Sales teams waste hours hunting for prospect contacts across scattered sources. This skill guides Claude to produce a structured CSV of company names, contacts, phones, and addresses based on your region and industry inputs.
actual_capabilities: Generate prospect lists scoped by user-supplied region and industry parameters.
Structure output as CSV with company name, contact, phone, and address columns.
Suggest candidate firms based on publicly known industry directories and the assistant's knowledge.
Reformat user-provided raw lead data into clean tabular CSV form.
Provide outreach segmentation hints such as company size or sub-vertical when requested.
limitations: The skill itself contains no scraping code; it relies on the assistant's general knowledge and any data you supply.
Generated contact details may be outdated or inaccurate and must be verified before use.
Output volume is limited by conversation context and cannot replace a dedicated database.
Users are responsible for compliance with privacy and anti-spam regulations.

use_cases.title: Outbound sales prospecting
use_cases.description: A sales representative requests a list of mid-sized manufacturers in a target region and receives a CSV ready for CRM import.

use_cases.title: Market sizing research
use_cases.description: An analyst gathers a sample list of firms in a niche industry to estimate market scope and identify key players.

use_cases.title: Event and trade show invitations
use_cases.description: An event organizer compiles a regional list of relevant companies for direct invitations to a trade show or webinar.

prompt_templates.title: Basic region and industry list
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a CSV of leads for [industry] in [region]. Columns: company name, contact, phone, address.

prompt_templates.title: Filtered prospect list
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce 20 leads for solar installers in California. Exclude companies with fewer than 10 employees. CSV columns: company, website, contact, phone, city.

prompt_templates.title: Verification-friendly export
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a CSV of 15 logistics firms in Guangdong. Add a column called source_hint that names the directory or website where the entry was inferred from. Mark uncertain rows with a verify flag.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adds provenance fields for human review

prompt_templates.title: Multi-segment outreach plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three CSV blocks for medical device distributors in Germany, France, and Italy. Each block has 10 rows with company, contact title, phone, email pattern, and a suggested opening line in the local language.

output_examples.input: Generate 5 leads for coffee roasters in Seattle as CSV.
output_examples.output: A CSV table with five rows. Each row lists a coffee roaster name, a contact role such as owner or buyer, a phone number placeholder, and a street address in the Seattle metropolitan area.

output_examples.input: List 10 SaaS companies in Berlin with website and contact role.
output_examples.output: A CSV with ten Berlin-based SaaS firms. Columns include company, website, contact role, and phone. Rows that cannot be confirmed are marked for manual verification.

best_practices: Always verify generated contact details against an official source before outreach.
Specify clear filters such as company size, sub-industry, and required columns to improve list quality.
Respect privacy and anti-spam laws by limiting use to legitimate business communication.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated phone numbers and emails as verified records.
Do not use the output for unsolicited bulk messaging or spam campaigns.
Do not request personal contact data of private individuals; the skill is intended for business contacts only.

faq.question: Does this skill perform live web scraping?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides only a prompt template and description. It does not include scraping code or network access.

faq.question: Where do the contact details come from?
faq.answer: They come from the assistant's general knowledge and any data you paste into the conversation. Treat them as drafts that need human verification.

faq.question: Is the output accurate enough for direct outreach?
faq.answer: No. Always verify each phone number, email, and address with an authoritative source before contacting anyone.

faq.question: Which file formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill is tuned for CSV output. You can ask the assistant to convert the result to Markdown tables or JSON if needed.

faq.question: Is this skill compliant with privacy regulations?
faq.answer: Compliance depends on how you use the output. You must follow local rules such as GDPR, CCPA, or PIPL when handling personal data.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The skill is documented for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It works in any environment that can read a Markdown skill file.
